briefing.incidentSummary:
A company's internal monitoring system flagged suspicious activity on an employee's laptop.

Confidential files were accessed at 2:00 AM — while the office was closed and no one was supposed to be working.

The laptop was seized and a forensic disk image was created. No obvious malware. No signs of remote access at first glance.

But the file system timestamps don't lie. Something happened at 2 AM — and someone was responsible.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Analyse file timestamps to isolate the 2 AM activity cluster

› Reconstruct the exact sequence of events during the breach window

› Determine whether access was local or remote

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Employee (Laptop Owner)

suspicionTrack.reason: Primary user of the laptop — credentials used for the 01:55 login
File access and modification sequence matches a manual working session
No remote access confirmed — physical presence or credential sharing required

digitalFootprint.locationTrace: Home network SSID — residential ISP IP
digitalFootprint.vpnCheck: No VPN or RDP session detected at 01:55

suspects.profileNotes: Sole assigned user of STRAT-LAP-09. The 01:55 login used their domain credentials from a residential IP matching their registered home address. Files accessed and modified belong exclusively to their project portfolio. No VPN or remote desktop session was active — the machine was operated locally. The employee claims they were asleep and did not access the laptop. However, no other individual is confirmed to have had physical access to the device.
suspects.description: Employee (Laptop Owner) is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: IT Administrator

suspicionTrack.reason: Has system-level access and could theoretically initiate a remote session
No RDP or admin remote session logged to STRAT-LAP-09 at the relevant time
Remote access test confirms no admin session was active at 01:55

digitalFootprint.deviceUsage: Admin console — no remote session to STRAT-LAP-09 logged
digitalFootprint.locationTrace: IT Department — no after-hours badge entry
digitalFootprint.vpnCheck: No admin VPN session to target machine

suspects.profileNotes: Has administrative remote access capability but no session to STRAT-LAP-09 is logged during the 2 AM window. Building access logs confirm the IT Administrator did not enter the office that night. Cannot have operated the laptop physically.
suspects.description: IT Administrator is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Remote Attacker

suspicionTrack.reason: Unusual after-hours access always warrants consideration of external intrusion
File modification pattern is consistent with a human-operated session, not automated exfiltration
No VPN, RDP, or inbound network session detected — remote access conclusively eliminated

suspicionTrack.label: Very Low — eliminated by network analysis

digitalFootprint.ipActivity: No external IPs detected on STRAT-LAP-09 at 01:55
digitalFootprint.deviceUsage: No RDP, SSH, or VPN tunnel active

suspects.profileNotes: Network logs and the remote access test both confirm no inbound connection was active during the 2 AM window. A remote attacker would require a VPN tunnel, RDP session, or other network channel — none of which are present. The session was conducted locally on the physical device.
suspects.description: Remote Attacker is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Credential Sharing — Unknown Party

suspicionTrack.reason: Employee denies access — raises possibility of shared or borrowed credentials
Physical access to the laptop would be required given no remote session
No evidence of a third party present at the residential address — remains unconfirmed

suspicionTrack.label: Moderate — cannot be fully ruled out without further investigation

digitalFootprint.ipActivity: Same residential IP as employee — local device

suspects.profileNotes: If the employee genuinely was not operating the laptop, a third party with physical access to the device and knowledge of the login credentials would be required. No evidence currently confirms or eliminates a household member or guest. Remains a secondary hypothesis pending further investigation.
suspects.description: Credential Sharing — Unknown Party is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

items.label: File Timestamp Analysis — 2 AM Activity Cluster
items.detail: File system timestamp analysis of STRAT-LAP-09 reveals two confidential documents with anomalous last-modified and last-accessed times clustering around 2:00 AM. report_draft.docx — Created 21:10, Last Modified 01:58, Last Accessed 02:01. budget_plan.xlsx — Created 20:45, Last Modified 02:03, Last Accessed 02:04. Both files were created during normal working hours but accessed and modified well after midnight. Neither file had been touched since their creation date until this event. The modification of both files — not just read access — indicates active editing, not passive browsing.

minigame.title: Timestamp Anomaly Detector
minigame.description: Compare the Created, Modified, and Accessed timestamps across both files. Flag all timestamps that fall within the suspicious 2 AM activity window (01:50–02:10).
minigame.hint: The suspicious window is 01:50 to 02:10. Any timestamp within that range should be flagged.
hints: Created timestamps are normal — the files existed before tonight. Focus on Modified and Accessed fields.
Four timestamps fall inside the 2 AM window: Modified and Accessed for both files.

onSuccess.message: ANOMALY CONFIRMED — Four timestamps cluster tightly in the 01:58–02:04 window. Both files were actively edited, not just opened. This is a working session, not a passive read.

items.label: Accessed Document Records — Confidential Files
items.detail: Both files accessed during the 2 AM window are classified as confidential. report_draft.docx contains draft findings from an ongoing internal strategy review — marked for senior management only. budget_plan.xlsx contains department-level budget allocations and projected headcount for the next fiscal year — restricted access, finance and C-suite only. The employee has legitimate access to both documents by role. However, accessing and editing confidential documents at 2 AM without business justification or prior notification is a policy violation regardless of access rights.

rows.value: Internal strategy review — Senior Management Only
Budget allocations — Finance and C-suite restricted
Accessing restricted files outside hours requires notification — none filed

items.label: System Activity Log — 01:55–02:06
items.detail: System event logs for STRAT-LAP-09 record the following sequence: 01:55 — Domain login: CORP\sanalyst (employee account). 02:01 — report_draft.docx opened in Microsoft Word. 02:03 — budget_plan.xlsx opened in Microsoft Excel. 02:03 — report_draft.docx saved (modified). 02:04 — budget_plan.xlsx saved (modified). 02:06 — Both applications closed. The login preceded all file activity by 6 minutes, consistent with a user settling into a working session. The save events confirm active editing. The orderly open-edit-save-close sequence is characteristic of deliberate manual operation rather than automated or scripted access.

minigame.title: Event Sequence Sorter
minigame.description: Arrange the system login, file open, file modification, and file close events in the correct chronological order to reconstruct the full 2 AM session.
minigame.hint: Use the timestamps to place each event in order. The login always comes first.
hints: Start with the login at 01:55. Everything else follows chronologically from there.
Both files were opened before either was saved. The close event is always last at 02:06.

onSuccess.message: SESSION RECONSTRUCTED — Login → open two files → edit and save both → close. This is an 11-minute deliberate working session at 2 AM. The sequence rules out accidental access or automated scripts.

items.label: Remote vs Local Access — No External Session Detected
items.detail: Network traffic analysis of STRAT-LAP-09 during the 01:50–02:10 window confirms: no VPN tunnel was active, no RDP inbound connection was recorded, no SSH session was established, and no remote management tool (TeamViewer, AnyDesk, etc.) was running. The machine was connected to a residential WiFi network (SSID: HOME-NET-07) via the employee's registered home broadband. The IP address (192.168.1.104) is a local NAT address consistent with a home router. The session was conducted locally — meaning someone physically present at the device performed the activity.

timeline.title: Files Created — Normal Hours
timeline.description: report_draft.docx and budget_plan.xlsx created during standard working hours. No prior after-hours access.

timeline.title: Late-Night Login
timeline.description: Domain login recorded on STRAT-LAP-09 using employee credentials. Residential IP — local device.

timeline.title: Confidential Files Accessed and Modified
timeline.description: Both restricted documents opened, edited, and saved within a 3-minute window.

timeline.title: Applications Closed
timeline.description: Word and Excel closed. Session ends. No further system activity recorded.

timeline.title: Monitoring Alert Triggered
timeline.description: Company monitoring system flags after-hours file access. IT notified. Laptop seized for forensic imaging.

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: The laptop owner confirmed — conducted an unauthorised 2 AM working session on restricted files with no remote access possible.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: Correct suspect, but the timeline and remote access elimination need to be fully documented.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: Network analysis eliminated the remote attacker. The session was local — pointing to the laptop owner.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

coldCase.title: Case Gone Cold
coldCase.subtitle: No evidence collected. The 2 AM session goes unexplained.
coldCase.label: COLD CASE
